<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The analysis of both directories is now complete.

Registration contains roughly 200 files and serves as the largest feature slice. BirthDeclaration and ChangeOfAddress each hold over 50 files. Shared infrastructure includes _Imports.razor and OfficerDisplayNames, which is reused across features. The report will list design system components with paths and line counts, map the vertical slice architecture, and catalog repeated Razor markup patterns with concrete duplication examples and line counts.

Each use-case folder follows a consistent backend layout: an Endpoint, Handler, Validator, and request/response DTOs. Shared backend utilities like CaseGuard, CaseAuthorization, document operations, and checklist mapping are duplicated across features. UI patterns include a nested page header hierarchy and a standard case list table with loading, empty, search, and filter states.

Case detail pages follow a fixed layout: loading state, header with lock actions, read-only alert, a 7/5 MudGrid split, and collapsible sections. Step components alternate between edit and read modes using the same AppCard, AppFormSection, and AppSaveCancelBar pattern. Decision sections duplicate a MudList, status AppAlert, and action buttons across three files. Document panels repeat upload, list, and preview markup across features. Four NR search dialog variants appear throughout. Estimated duplication: document panels (~90 lines), decision sections (~25 lines checklist markup).

Upload components duplicate roughly 25 lines of file upload UI. Case list pages repeat an 80–100 line filter, search, and table shell. The NR search dialog’s Search() method spans about 20 lines and appears in four variants.
</details></think>

---

## 2. Features Structure & Common Patterns

**Root:** `/Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality/src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/Features/`

```
Features/
├── _Imports.razor                    # Shared Razor imports (DesignSystem + Validation)
├── Registration/                     # ~200 files — largest domain
│   ├── Pages/                        # 7 routable pages
│   ├── Components/                   # 18 step/section/dialog components
│   └── {UseCase}/                    # e.g. RecordIdentity/, ApproveCase/
│       ├── *Endpoint.cs
│       ├── *Handler.cs
│       ├── *Validator.cs
│       └── *Request/Response/Dto.cs
├── BirthDeclaration/                 # ~60 files
│   ├── Pages/ (2), Components/ (5)
│   └── BirthDeclarationEndpoints.cs
└── ChangeOfAddress/                  # ~55 files
    ├── Pages/ (2), Components/ (3)
    └── ChangeOfAddressEndpoints.cs
```

### Vertical-slice backend pattern (all three features)

Each use case is a folder with:
- **`*Endpoint.cs`** — static Minimal API handler, validates, maps HTTP → handler
- **`*Handler.cs`** — injected service with `Handle(...)` method
- **`*Validator.cs`** — FluentValidation `AbstractValidator<T>`
- Aggregators: `BirthDeclarationEndpoints.cs`, `ChangeOfAddressEndpoints.cs`, plus Registration endpoints scattered similarly

### Cross-feature UI patterns

| Pattern | Where used | Key files |
|---------|------------|-----------|
| **Case list page** | All 3 | `RegistrationCaseList.razor` (271L), `BirthDeclarationCaseList.razor` (209L), `ChangeOfAddressCaseList.razor` (270L) |
| **Case detail page** | All 3 | `RegistrationCaseDetail.razor` (930L), `BirthDeclarationCaseDetail.razor` (374L), `ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor` (429L) |
| **Officer decision section** | All 3 | `CaseDecisionSection.razor` (257L), `BirthDeclarationDecisionSection.razor` (180L), `ChangeOfAddressDecisionSection.razor` (126L) |
| **Document panel** | Registration + BirthDeclaration | `RegistrationCaseDocumentPanel.razor` (178L), `BirthDeclarationDocumentPanel.razor` (180L) |
| **Edit/read step cards** | Registration only | `AddressStep`, `CivilStatusStep`, `ResidenceStep`, `HouseholdStep`, `BirthInformationStep` |
| **Claim/release lock** | All 3 detail pages | Take case / Release lock buttons + read-only alert |
| **Checklist mappers** | All 3 | `CaseReviewChecklistMapper.cs`, `BirthDeclarationChecklistMapper` (in `GetBirthDeclarationChecklistHandler.cs`), `ChangeOfAddressChecklistMapper.cs` |

### Registration-specific patterns (not yet in other features)

- Multi-step intake via `AppCollapsibleSection` + `RegistrationIntakeStepSummaries.cs`
- Wizard steps: `AddressStep.razor` (362L), `ResidenceStep.razor` (621L), `CivilStatusStep.razor` (316L), etc.
- Police verification, duplicate investigation, certificates

---

## 3. Shared Infrastructure

### Validation (`/Validation/`)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `FluentMudValidation.cs` (37L) | Bridges FluentValidation → MudBlazor `Validation` delegate via `ToMudValidateValue<TRequest, TForm>()` |
| `ValidationResults.cs` (18L) | Maps FluentValidation results → `Results.ValidationProblem()` for API endpoints |

Used in 7 Razor files: `CivilStatusStep`, `HouseholdStep`, `ResidenceStep`, `RegistrationCaseDetail`, `BirthDeclarationCaseDetail`, `ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail`.

### Cross-feature helpers (under Registration, used everywhere)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `Features/Registration/OfficerDisplayNames.cs` | Officer name + lock status display |
| `Features/Registration/Components/RegistrationRoleGate.razor` (28L) | Role-based page gate; wraps all case lists and Birth/CoA detail pages |
| `Features/Registration/SearchNationalRegister/` | Shared NR search handler used by Registration, BirthDeclaration, ChangeOfAddress dialogs |

### Per-feature authorization/guards (parallel structure)

| Feature | Guard | Authorization |
|---------|-------|---------------|
| Registration | `RegistrationCaseGuard` | `RegistrationCaseAuthorization.cs` (104L) |
| BirthDeclaration | `BirthDeclarationCaseGuard.cs` (35L) | `BirthDeclarationCaseAuthorization` |
| ChangeOfAddress | `ChangeOfAddressCaseGuard.cs` (35L) | `ChangeOfAddressCaseAuthorization` |

Guards are nearly identical: `GetForEditAsync` / `GetForViewAsync` → load from repo → `authorization.EnsureCanEdit/View`.

### Dialog infrastructure

- `IAppDialogService` / `AppDialogService` — confirm dialogs with consistent options
- `AppDialogOptions.Small/Medium/Large` — used in decision sections and list pages

### No shared base classes for UI

Step components and decision sections are standalone `.razor` files with duplicated `@code` patterns rather than inheritance.

---

## 4. Repeated Razor Markup — Concrete Duplication

### A. Document preview panel (~90 lines duplicated)

`RegistrationCaseDocumentPanel.razor` and `BirthDeclarationDocumentPanel.razor` share:
- Same CSS class `registration-case-document-panel`
- Identical document list markup (lines 17–53 vs 17–54)
- Identical preview block with image/PDF/empty (lines 56–93 vs 58–94)
- Identical `@code` helpers: `SelectDocument`, `GetListItemClass`, `IsPdf`, `IsImage` (lines 128–177 vs 128–179)

Only differences: upload component wrapper, API URL path, empty-state text, document type in list label.

### B. Officer decision checklist (~25 lines × 3 files)

All three `*DecisionSection.razor` files repeat:

```razor
<MudList T="string" Dense="true" Class="mb-4">
    @foreach (var question in _checklist.Questions)
    {
        <MudListItem Icon="@(question.IsSatisfied ? CheckCircle : RadioButtonUnchecked)">
            <MudText Typo="Typo.body2">@question.Question</MudText>
            @if (!question.IsSatisfied && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(question.BlockingReason))
            {
                <MudText Typo="Typo.caption" Color="Color.Secondary">@question.BlockingReason</MudText>
            }
        </MudListItem>
    }
</MudList>
```

- `CaseDecisionSection.razor` lines 28–43
- `BirthDeclarationDecisionSection.razor` lines 19–30
- `ChangeOfAddressDecisionSection.razor` lines 14–25

Rejected/suspended status alerts also follow the same template (lines 54–65 Registration, 39–46 Birth, 34–43 CoA).

### C. File upload UI (~25 lines × 2 files)

`DocumentUpload.razor` (87L) and `MedicalDeclarationUpload.razor` (76L) share lines 15–34:

```razor
<MudFileUpload T="IBrowserFile" Accept=".pdf,.jpg,.jpeg,.png" FilesChanged="OnFileSelected">
    <ActivatorContent>
        <MudButton ... StartIcon="@Icons.Material.Outlined.Upload">Choose file</MudButton>
    </ActivatorContent>
</MudFileUpload>
@if (_selectedFile is not null) { ... }
<MudButton ... Disabled="_uploading || _selectedFile is null" OnClick="Upload">Upload</MudButton>
```

Plus identical upload `@code` pattern (stream open, snackbar, `_uploading` flag).

### D. Case list shell (~80–100 lines × 3 files)

All three list pages repeat this structure:

1. `RegistrationRoleGate` wrapper
2. `AppPage` → `AppPageHeader` → `AppSection`
3. `_loading` → `AppLoading Kind="Skeleton"`
4. Empty → `AppEmptyState` with optional action button
5. `AppSearchBar` + filter `MudButton` row (`GetFilterVariant` / `GetFilterColor`)
6. `AppDataTable` with Case ID link (`[..8]…`), Status chip, Officer lock status, Opened date

Filter enum + `ApplyFilter()` + `GetEmptyFilterDescription()` are copy-pasted in each file's `@code` block (~60 lines each).

### E. National Register search dialogs (~20 lines Search + table × 4 files)

Four dialogs share the same search form and `Search()` method:

| File | Lines | Action on match |
|------|-------|-----------------|
| `NationalRegisterSearchDialog.razor` | 218 | Link to registration case |
| `OpenChangeOfAddressDialog.razor` | 138 | Open CoA case |
| `ParentLinkDialog.razor` | 138 | Link parent |
| (partial overlap) | | |

Shared block (example from `OpenChangeOfAddressDialog.razor` lines 15–30):

```razor
<MudTextField @bind-Value="_givenName" Label="Given name" />
<MudTextField @bind-Value="_familyName" Label="Family name" />
<AppDateField @bind-Value="_birthDate" Label="Date of birth" MaxDate="@DateTime.Today" Clearable="true" />
<MudButton ... OnClick="Search" Disabled="_searching">Search</MudButton>
```

Identical `Search()` body calling `SearchNationalRegisterHandler.Handle(new SearchNationalRegisterRequest(...))` in all four.

### F. Edit/read card pattern (~40 lines × 5 Registration steps)

`CivilStatusStep.razor` exemplifies the pattern used across `AddressStep`, `ResidenceStep`, `HouseholdStep`, `BirthInformationStep`:

- **Edit mode:** `AppCard` → `MudForm` + `ToMudValidateValue` → `AppFormSection` → `AppSaveCancelBar`
- **Read mode:** `AppCard` with `HeaderActions` Edit button → display values

Example from `CivilStatusStep.razor`:
- Edit: lines 16–82
- Read: lines 84–127

`ResidenceStep.razor` (621L) repeats this three times (category, permit, immigration decision).

### G. Case detail header + lock UX (~30 lines × 3 files)

Birth and ChangeOfAddress detail pages share nearly identical header/lock markup:

```razor
@if (_case.IsReadOnlyDueToLock) {
    <MudButton Variant="Variant.Outlined" OnClick="TakeCase">Take case</MudButton>
} else if (_case.CanEdit) {
    <MudButton Variant="Variant.Outlined" OnClick="ReleaseLock">Release lock</MudButton>
}
<MudButton StartIcon="ArrowBack" ...>Back to list</MudButton>

@if (_case.IsReadOnlyDueToLock) {
    <AppAlert Severity="Warning" Title="Read-only">This case is locked to another officer...</AppAlert>
}
```

- `BirthDeclarationCaseDetail.razor` lines 47–70
- `ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor` lines 50–73

Registration has a richer variant (lines 62–116) with more explicit lock messaging.

---

## Summary: Duplication Hotspots

| Hotspot | Files | Est. duplicated lines |
|---------|-------|----------------------|
| Document list + preview panel | 2 | ~90 |
| Case list page shell + filters | 3 | ~240 total (~80 each) |
| Decision checklist + status alerts | 3 | ~75 total (~25 each) |
| NR search dialog | 4 | ~80 total (~20 each) |
| File upload card | 2 | ~50 |
| Edit/read step card pattern | 5 | ~200 total (~40 each) |
| Case detail lock header | 3 | ~60 total (~20 each) |
| `*CaseGuard.cs` | 3 | ~30 each (backend) |
| `AttachDocumentHandler` | 3 features | ~25 lines storage/save pattern |

**Total rough estimate:** 400–600 lines of repeated Razor (plus parallel backend handlers/endpoints per feature).

The design system is well-established for page layout and form chrome; the main consolidation opportunities are **case list pages**, **document panels**, **decision sections**, **NR search dialogs**, and the **edit/read step card** pattern — none of which have been lifted into shared feature-level components yet.
